paper_title: LoRA-Switch: Boosting the Efficiency of Dynamic LLM Adapters via System-Algorithm Co-design

problem.latency_overhead: Dynamic adapters increase decoding inference latency by 250-950%.
problem.parameter_increase: The impact on parameter size is minor, typically adding only 1-5% of the origin model.
problem.root_cause: The latency is primarily attributed to the prolonged execution time of context operations during CUDA kernel runs, caused by four or more additional CUDA kernel calls for each layer.

proposed_solution.mechanism: Each token is associated with k weighted paths of LoRA adapters, which are activated prior to the decoding of the token.

limitations_of_existing_approaches.routing_structures: Existing dynamic adapters use block-wise or layer-wise routing structures.
limitations_of_existing_approaches.merging_problem: Pre-merging activated LoRA adapters into backbone weights is not feasible because it requires unmerging them for the next input, making the routing dynamicity prohibitively costly.
limitations_of_existing_approaches.moe_overhead: The fragmented operational approach of MoE, where each layer waits for the router, introduces greater overhead costs.

title: LoRA-Switch: Boosting the Efficiency of Dynamic LLM Adapters via System-Algorithm Co-design
